summary: Health Monitor — alerting on connector sync failures (PagerDuty + email)
description_md:
As a workspace-admin, I want the Health Monitor to automatically alert me via PagerDuty and email when connector sync failures occur, so that I can quickly respond to data pipeline issues and maintain data freshness across my tenant.

When a connector (Salesforce, SAP S/4) experiences sync failures, it can silently impact downstream reporting and compliance processes. The workspace-admin needs immediate notification to investigate and remediate before data becomes stale beyond acceptable retention windows.

**Acceptance Criteria**:

1. When a connector sync fails with HTTP 5xx error, an alert is created in the Health Monitor with severity "Critical" and includes the connector name, data source, and error details.

2. A PagerDuty incident is triggered within 2 minutes of the alert creation, with the incident linked to the tenant ID and connector identifier in the payload.

3. An email notification is sent to the workspace-admin with subject line "[Connector Alert] {connector_name} sync failure" containing a direct link to the Health Monitor dashboard.

4. Alerts are deduplicated such that repeated failures of the same connector within a 15-minute window do not create duplicate PagerDuty incidents or email notifications.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Starting implementation. I'll use the existing connector health check infrastructure and add PagerDuty integration via their Events API v2. Need to confirm with the team what severity level to use for different error types.

comments.author: dev1@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: For severity mapping: HTTP 5xx = Critical, HTTP 4xx = Warning, timeout = Critical. Also, make sure the deduplication window is configurable per tenant in case some customers want different alerting behavior.
